channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Strange interstellar comet speculated to be alien spacecraft is older than Solar System - Yahoo
item.description:
1. Strange interstellar comet speculated to be alien spacecraft is older than Solar System  Yahoo
  2. NASA’s Webb Finds Clues to Ancient, Distant Origin of Comet 3I/ATLAS  NASA Science (.gov)
  3. Interstellar comet that zoomed past Earth could be oldest and coldest object ever seen in solar system, astronomers say  CBS News
  4. Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS is almost as old as the universe itself  Scientific American
  5. Was Methane a Signature of Life in 3I/ATLAS?  Avi Loeb – Medium

item.title: 1st-of-its-kind mission will attempt to save aging space telescope using robot spacecraft - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: This Famously Pink Planet Is Hiding a Surprising Secret - Gizmodo
item.description:
1. This Famously Pink Planet Is Hiding a Surprising Secret  Gizmodo
  2. Webb telescope finds possible salt clouds on a pink alien world  Mashable
  3. "Pink Planet" is surrounded by salty clouds, researchers using Webb telescope find  CBS News
  4. For a decade the Pink Planet was too faint for any ground telescope to study — and when James Webb finally caught its spectrum, the best fit to the light was an atmosphere hazed with salt clouds  Space Daily
  5. Meet the exotic Pink Planet with salty clouds  EarthSky

item.title: June Bootids meteor shower to peak this week - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description:
1. June Bootids meteor shower to peak this week  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  2. Meteor Activity Outlook for June 20-26, 2026  American Meteor Society
  3. The June Bootids have flared to dozens of meteors an hour in rare years like 1998, but most years the shower is so weak it shows almost nothing — and no outburst is forecast for 2026  Space Daily
  4. The most unpredictable meteor shower of the year peaks this week. Here's what to expect  Space
  5. June Bootids: Most chaotic meteor shower of 2026 to peak this week  The Independent
item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station can see flashes of light even with their eyes closed — not from light entering the eye, but from high-energy space particles passing through their body and triggering the retina or visual pathway directly. - Space Daily
item.description: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station can see flashes of light even with their eyes closed — not from light entering the eye, but from high-energy space particles passing through their body and triggering the retina or visual pathway directly.  Space Daily

item.title: A hidden fungal network longer than the distance to the Sun wraps the roots of 70% of Earth's plants, and the first global map finds it densest not under rainforests but wild grasslands - Earth.com
item.description:
1. A hidden fungal network longer than the distance to the Sun wraps the roots of 70% of Earth's plants, and the first global map finds it densest not under rainforests but wild grasslands  Earth.com

  3. A Hidden Universe: Underground Fungi Could Stretch to the Sun Nearly a Billion Times  atmos.earth

  5. Study: Fungal Networks So Massive They’d Span 10% of the Milky Way  Answers in Genesis

item.title: Europa’s surface is almost too clean. In a Solar System full of impactors, an icy moon should accumulate scars for billions of years — yet Europa has very few. The simplest explanation is also the strangest: the surface we see is young, constantly rewritten by fo - Space Daily

item.title: On March 13, 1989, a geomagnetic storm collapsed Quebec's entire power grid in just 90 seconds — leaving 6 million people in the cold and dark before most of them had even woken up - Space Daily
item.description:
1. On March 13, 1989, a geomagnetic storm collapsed Quebec's entire power grid in just 90 seconds — leaving 6 million people in the cold and dark before most of them had even woken up  Space Daily
  2. How to Protect Earth Against Violent Space Weather  Nautilus | Science
  3. Solar storms don't harm people directly — they induce electric currents in long metal, meaning the power lines, pipelines and undersea internet cables we've strung across the planet are exactly what they attack  Space Daily
  4. GWYNNE DYER: Solar storms: one thing less to worry about?  Windsor Star
  5. Scientists Propose Creating a Space Shield Against Solar Storms  Zamin.uz

item.title: T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find - ScienceDaily
item.description:
1. T. rex took 40 years to reach full size, scientists find  ScienceDaily
  2. T. rex may have needed up to 40 years to reach its full eight-ton size, and that slow-burn growth could help explain why the king of dinosaurs ruled for so long, as per scientists  The Times of India
  3. From birth to adulthood, T. rex took 40 years growing into an eight-ton giant, and scientists think that l  The Economic Times
  4. Tyrannosaurus Rex Didn't Reach Full Size Until Age 40, New Study Reveals  inkorr.com

item.title: For 400 years, sailors crossing parts of the Indian Ocean have reported patches of sea glowing pale white from horizon to horizon, until satellite imagery finally captured the phenomenon and suggested it is likely the light of trillions of microscopic bioluminesce - Space Daily

item.title: A spacecraft no larger than a small car, launched the year Star Wars came out, is still moving outward at 38,000 miles per hour far beyond the orbit of Pluto — and the radio signals it sends home, traveling at the speed of light, now take more than 23 hours to re - Space Daily
item.description:
1. A spacecraft no larger than a small car, launched the year Star Wars came out, is still moving outward at 38,000 miles per hour far beyond the orbit of Pluto — and the radio signals it sends home, traveling at the speed of light, now take more than 23 hours to re  Space Daily
  2. Voyager 1 has been travelling non-stop since 1977, faster than a speeding bullet every second of every day, and it has not yet reached the distance of a single light-year from Earth  Space Daily
  3. It's Official: Voyager 1 Will Break A Cosmic Record, Reaching 1 Light-Day From Earth On November 18, 2026  IFLScience
  4. The 49-Year-Old Spacecraft Still Talking From Deep Space  HackerNoon
  5. NASA Launched It In 1977, Nearly 50 Years Later, It's Still Talking To Earth | Viral News  News18

  4. "Once you show regeneration can be activated, it opens the door to new questions..." Mammals – including us – could regrow limbs say scientists  BBC Wildlife Magazine
  5. Scientists Discover Gene That One Day Could Help Humans Regrow Lost Limbs  ColombiaOne.com

item.title: For decades the heavy rocks that carve trails across a Death Valley lakebed were never seen moving, until cameras caught them gliding on thin rafts of melting ice pushed by a light wind - Space Daily
item.description: For decades the heavy rocks that carve trails across a Death Valley lakebed were never seen moving, until cameras caught them gliding on thin rafts of melting ice pushed by a light wind  Space DailySee more headlines & perspectives on Google News

item.title: Researchers at the University of California Riverside found in May 2026 that living systems distribute their amino acids more evenly than non-living chemistry does — a statistical pattern subtle enough that it went unnoticed for decades and powerful enough to - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Researchers at the University of California Riverside found in May 2026 that living systems distribute their amino acids more evenly than non-living chemistry does — a statistical pattern subtle enough that it went unnoticed for decades and powerful enough to  Space Daily
  2. Contact? Groundbreaking Israeli method may soon detect life in outer space  The Times of Israel
  3. Researchers use molecular diversity to distinguish possible signs of life  AzerNews
  4. Israeli, US scientists propose new approach to detect life on other worlds  JNS

item.title: Most of an octopus's brain cells live in its arms, not in its head — only about 8 percent of its 500 million neurons sit inside the central brain, with the rest spread across the eight arms and the optic lobes behind its eyes, in a body design some neuroscientists des - Space Daily
item.description:
1. Most of an octopus's brain cells live in its arms, not in its head — only about 8 percent of its 500 million neurons sit inside the central brain, with the rest spread across the eight arms and the optic lobes behind its eyes, in a body design some neuroscientists des  Space Daily
  2. This shell-less ocean creature evolved 140 million years ago: Scientists say it is the closest thing to an alien on Earth  The Times of India
  3. They have nine brains, three hearts and they dream: interacting with an octopus is the closest thing to meeting an alien  yourweather.co.uk

item.title: 6 Things Landscaping Pros Do to Keep Bugs and Animals From Burrowing in Their Yards Summer After Summer - The Spruce

item.title: Webb spots the birth of a giant galaxy and a supermassive black hole - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Webb spots the birth of a giant galaxy and a supermassive black hole  Phys.org
  2. Another Early Universe Surprise from the JWST: A Mature Galaxy Cluster  Universe Today
  3. James Webb Space Telescope finds most distant galaxy cluster with gravitational lensing  eciks.org
  4. When JWST looked at XLSSC 122, a galaxy cluster 10.4 billion light-years away, it caught gravity bending light from galaxies about 12 billion years in the past, and a dark matter core packed too tightly for cosmic noon  Space Daily
  5. New JWST images of abnormally well-developed galaxy cluster open up the 'cosmic noon' frontier  Phys.org

item.title: Einstein Probe detects mysterious X-ray transient that doesn't fit any known class - Phys.org
item.description:
1. Einstein Probe detects mysterious X-ray transient that doesn't fit any known class  Phys.org
  2. Astronomers may have solved the mystery of cosmic X-ray flashes  Tech Explorist
  3. Fast X-Ray Transient, Meaning, Sources, Latest News  Vajiram & Ravi
  4. Astronomers Uncover Clues Behind Powerful Cosmic X-Ray Flashes  DD News
  5. Astronomers of Indian Institute of Astrophysics Reveal Origin of Energetic Cosmic X-ray Flashes  Argus English
